hero.subtitle: Hand a clip to AI vision, get a written script in the tone you pick, choose from a dozen neural voices, and render the final video — trim, voiceover and burned-in captions in a single pass.

preview.title: Studio stack
preview.body: Vision-driven script, twelve Piper voices, frame-precise trim and merge, and one-click render with optional captions — server-side power, glass-clear UI.
preview.hint: Local vision LLM · 12 Piper voices · 8 script tones · FFmpeg render

features.title: A console, not a toolbox
features.body: Every step lives on one canvas. Drop, generate, listen, trim, merge, render — without bouncing between tools or losing your edit decisions.

workflow.title: Four beats. One timeline.
workflow.subtitle: Each step is isolated, inspectable, and built for iteration.
workflow.step1Body: Upload MP4, MOV, or WebM up to 200 MB — single clips or a library of up to eight you can reorder and merge. Files are processed on the server and cleaned up after export.
workflow.step2Body: A local MiniCPM-V vision model watches keyframes and writes a narration in the tone you pick — cinematic, documentary, demo, and more. Paste a README or product notes (up to 4 000 characters) for extra context, then edit every line inline.
workflow.step3Body: Choose from twelve Piper neural voices spanning US and UK accents — preview each on a sample line, regenerate freely, and sync timestamps to your timeline.
workflow.step4Body: One FFmpeg pass mixes voiceover, optional burned-in captions, and your trim points into the final MP4 — no chained round trips.

stats.title: Built for production floors
stats.l1: Render mixes voice, captions, and trim
stats.l3: Vision scripting and TTS run entirely on your machine

specs.title: What's inside
specs.body: A focused stack for narrated short-form: vision scripting, neural voiceover, captions, trim and merge — all wired to one render endpoint.
specs.c1b: A local vision LLM samples keyframes from your clip and drafts a script in one of eight tones — cinematic, documentary, demo, energetic, calm, educational, promotional, or your own custom prompt. Feed it a README or product notes for extra context, then edit every line before voicing.
specs.c2b: A dozen Piper voices across US and UK accents — Amy, Ryan, Alan, Jenny, Lessac, Kathleen, Joe, John, Norman, LJ, Alba and Cori. Preview each on a sample line, then bake the winner into the final render. All synthesis runs offline.
specs.c3b: Sync timestamps to the loaded video with one click, or edit segment in/out times directly in the Transcript tab — neighbours cascade so the whole caption track stays contiguous.
specs.c4b: Set in/out on the timeline with frame-precise handles, scrub or step the playhead, replace the active clip with a selection, or stitch up to eight clips together with per-clip trim handles — render emits one clean MP4.

faq.title: Signals & noise
faq.a1: Uploads live in an ephemeral session directory while ffmpeg runs, then get cleaned up — nothing is retained for training.
faq.a2: Entirely local: MiniCPM-V 2.6 (an Ollama-hosted vision LLM built for multi-image and video understanding) drafts the script, and Piper voices it. Captions come from the timed segments you edit in the Transcript tab — no cloud, no extra model.
faq.q3: Can I use my own voice?
faq.a3: Not yet — Studio ships twelve curated Piper voices across US and UK accents. Custom voice cloning isn't bundled.
faq.a4: It's a companion console for narrated short-form: scripting, voicing, captions, trim, and merge. Take the rendered MP4 into your NLE for final polish.

closing.title: Ready when your footage lands.
closing.body: Open Studio, drop a clip, and let vision write your next voiceover.
